description: Single automated match test. Events are logged by server/clients and scraped from logs. Events with an 'action' block are executed by the MockPlayer bot on the matching client.

events.description: Dedicated server starts and listens on port 8910
Client A connects to the server
Client B connects to the server
Game starts (World scene loads on both clients)
Server spawns two armies of 10 soldiers for each player
Player A selects its first army
Player A sends its first army to the Stables capture point
Player B selects its first army
Player B sends its first army to the Blacksmith capture point
Once A controls Stables, Player A flips all its armies from defensive to aggressive
Once B controls Blacksmith, Player B flips all its armies from defensive to aggressive
Game ends because only one player has any armies left (or the 120s match timeout fires as a safety net)

other_tests.description_of_test: Match did not finish via timeout safety net (TEST_GAME_OVER_TIMEOUT must NOT appear — its presence means combat failed to resolve within 120s)
Headless texture paths: red/blue spearman PNGs load as ImageTexture (same path logic as Unit3D)
Headless 3D client spawn path: instantiating World.tscn and calling _client_spawn_armies_impl produces the expected unit count
Headless 3D goal arrival: Army3D.move_army drives every soldier's sync_target to within 3px on XZ

ad_hoc_tests.description_of_test: Sprite facing flip: ask a client-side Unit3D to move right, then left, then stand still. Every time _facing_right changes the code prints 'TEST_FACING_FLIP: ...'. The headless harness asserts the material's UV flip is applied correctly (uv1_scale.x < 0 + uv1_offset.x = 1 when facing right, unflipped when facing left, and facing persists while idle). Not run by verify_test_logs.sh; invoke manually when you suspect the soldier sprites are not mirroring.
